title: betula nana var. alpestris
intro: Betula nana var. alpestris, also known as alpine dwarf birch, is a small shrub-like plant that belongs to the Betulaceae family and is native to the mountainous regions of Europe. The medicinal properties of Betula nana var. alpestris lie in its leaves, bark, and roots, which contain salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, making it a natural anti-inflammatory and pain reliever. The health benefits of this plant include reducing fever, alleviating headaches and menstrual cramps, and soothing skin irritations. Herbal preparations of Betula nana var. alpestris typically involve infusing the leaves and bark in hot water to make a tea, or using the plant's extract in salves and ointments to treat skin conditions.
